svg.title: Autonomous Compliance Fabric — hybrid GRC architecture for LinkedIn
svg.desc: Four-zone architecture diagram showing on-premise endpoints, compliance server with local AI, secure one-way relay through DMZ, and read-only cloud CISO dashboard. Data flows outbound only, signed and encrypted.
svg.text: Zone A — Corporate LAN
No external access — internal traffic only

g.text: Ed25519 SSH key stored in Vault

svg.text: Agents poll
Zone B — Compliance server (dedicated VM — Ubuntu 22.04)
8 vCPU · 32 GB RAM · 500 GB NVMe · all processing stays here

g.text: Retains: SHA-256 hash · score · fix cmd

svg.text: TLS 1.3
Perimeter boundary — signed + encrypted payload only crosses here — no raw data · no inbound connections
Zone C — DMZ relay (org-controlled)

g.text: No inbound rules exist · logs to SIEM

svg.text: Verified payload
Zone D — Cloud dashboard (read-only SaaS)
No write-back path · no access to on-prem · GET endpoints only
What the cloud sees
SHA-256 hashes of configs (not the configs)
Control pass / fail scores per framework
OS-specific remediation commands
No hostnames · no IPs · no raw data — ever
Key design decisions
LLM runs fully on-prem — Mistral 7B GGUF
Private key never leaves HashiCorp Vault
1 control mapped to N frameworks (dedup)
AI generates fix · human approves · agent verifies
